title: Lettre de Thomas R. McCabe à Léon Blum relative aux surplus de l'Armée et de la Marine destinés à être cédés à la France, ensemble trois annexes

ADDRESS OFFICUL COMMI

AATIQMI

THE SECRETARY OF STATI

WASHING

DEPARTMENT OF STATE

WASHINGTON

OFFICE OF FOREIGN LIGUIDATION CONMISSIONEL

May 29, 1946

Room 520 1818 H Strect N.W.

Dear Monsieur Blum:

Referrirg to the Agreement dated 28 May 1946, relating to the sale to Jour Governmerit of United States Army and Navy surplus, this will confirm to you that the latest estimates which we have receired from the Army with respect to the surplus property to be transferred under the agreement are as follows (at origiral cost):

As was pointed out to you during the negotistions, it is estimated that property represerting an origiral cost of approximstey $100,000,000 is already comitted for sale to various purchasers, in- The Honorable

Leon Blum

Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiory of the Provisional Government of the French Republic

In paragraph ? of the Agreement, provision has been made 10I°

the contirued use by the United States Argy and Navy of property in

France prior to its turnover under this Agreement. This provision 1s not intended to cover unusual or abnormal use or consumption.

If for

any reason, the use or consumption is materially greater than that now contemplated, it is envisaged thät an appropriate adjustmert will be made in the amount to be paid by the French Government under the

Agreemerit.

It is not irtended, however, that this provision will

necessitate adjustment in the case of a

minor excess or deficiency,

which is to be expected in dealing with estimates of this magnitude.

We have taken note of the items Listed in the "French Purchasing Program of United States Arny Surplus" submitted to the Foreign

Liquidation Connissioner by the French Council of Allied Surplus on

May 7, 1946, and subsequently amended, (Schedule ""). To the extent that these items are not transferred under the Agreement, the Foreign

Liquidation Cormissioner will use his best efforts to nake such itens available fron surplus stocks is. other parts of the world. These ¿tens wIll be made available at 20% of origiral cost. The Foreign

Licuidation Commissioner will also use his best efforts to arrange

Ior the transportation of such property to France, the cost thereof to be borne by the Fretch Government.

It is also the intention of the Foreign Licuidation Connissioner to nake available to the French Government such of Its re quirements ior locomotives and rolling stoc, as may be avariable trom

The Memorardum of Agreement provides for the termination of sales as of noon, Paris time, ley 25, 1940. For Jour convenience, we will be glad io request, Ur. Virden to communicate to the Conseii des

Surplus information as to the property covered by contrects or com mitments prior to that tine.

Enclosures: Schedules "A" through "O"

Sincerely yours,

Soman 77.4: Cance

THOMAS B. MCCABE

Connissioner
